expectations.text: model_and_parts_created: Script creates a model named 'crane' with three rigid body parts (tower, boom, payload)
expectations.evidence: 'model create model_name = crane' present; 'part create rigid_body name_and_position' for .crane.tower, .crane.boom, and .crane.payload all present.

expectations.text: markers_use_eval_loc: Boom and payload markers use eval() with loc_global() or loc_relative_to() to compute positions
markers_use_ori_along_axis: Both mkr_boom_tip and mkr_payload_hang use eval with ori_along_axis() with full dot-path marker names
eval_expressions_single_line: Every eval expression stays entirely on one line with no line continuation (&) inside it
expectations.evidence: All four eval() expressions (two location=, two orientation=) are each entirely on a single line. No '&' continuation appears inside any eval(...).

expectations.text: function_string_wrapped: The long FUNCTION= expression is split across multiple lines using comma-separated quoted strings
expectations.evidence: FUNCTION= is split into 14 comma-separated quoted string fragments each ending with '&', e.g. '"STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)", &' followed by '" + STEP5(...)" &' etc.

expectations.text: joints_correct: Two revolute joints (tower-ground, boom-tower) and one spherical joint (payload-boom) are created
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint revolute for .crane.jnt_tower_ground and .crane.jnt_boom_tower; constraint create joint spherical for .crane.jnt_payload_boom.

expectations.text: force_expression_correct: The force function includes STEP5 ramps, a VZ velocity-damping term, and an IMPACT contact term
mass_properties_uses_modify: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties
expectations.evidence: 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' used for all three parts (tower, boom, payload), each specifying mass, ixx/iyy/izz, and center_of_mass_marker.

expectations.text: dot_path_names: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: Every named object uses full paths such as .crane.tower, .crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.payload.cm, .crane.jnt_tower_ground, etc. No short names used.
grep for 'adams_id' in the script returns no matches.

expectations.text: geometry_present: Cylinder geometry on tower and boom, ellipsoid on payload
expectations.evidence: 'geometry create shape cylinder' for .crane.tower.cyl_body and .crane.boom.cyl_body; 'geometry create shape ellipsoid' for .crane.payload.ellip_body.

expectations.text: simulate_transient: Script uses simulation single_run transient for 3 seconds with 3000 steps
expectations.evidence: 'simulation single_run transient' with end_time = 3.0 and number_of_steps = 3000.

expectations.text: adams_view_no_errors: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: run_adams_cmd.py exited with code 0. The two 'ERROR:' occurrences in aview.log are inside Adams comment lines beginning with '!' (Python tracebacks in a comment block), not actual Adams error diagnostics.
